title: alpinia subverticillata
intro: Alpinia subverticillata, also known as the lesser galangal, is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Alpinia subverticillata include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's rhizome is often dried and ground into powder, used in teas, infusions, and capsules, or made into a paste for external applications, such as treating skin irritations and wounds.
